items.park_reason: Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix Tier-4 would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide tier-3 stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

Pure-C distance 1; a verified pure-C for-loop form retires all 3 regfix rules. Blocked by NEIGHBORING CHEATS, not by itself: its pure-C form renumbers .L labels file-wide, breaking func_8006BD28 + func_80070C70 asmfix delete_between/insert_before rules -- and those siblings are asm-INJECTION bridges (paste target asm verbatim via asmfix, not pure C). SOTN fix = RETIRE those sibling bridges to pure C (eliminating their label-anchored cheats); then this lands with nothing to break. Do NOT stabilize the sibling cheat anchors -- that hardens cheats, against Tier-4.

Canonical-asm (hand-written GTE wrapper), no pure-C form -> needs user canonical-asm sign-off. Body is mtc2 a0/a1/a2 -> nop -> avsz3 -> mfc2 v0; avsz3 (GTE avg-of-3-Z) + mtc2/mfc2 GTE reg moves have no C analog. target asm (asm/funcs/func_8007F10C.s) annotates every insn 'handwritten instruction' w/ glabel/endlabel. Engine misrouted to active w/ distance 1: the only stripped diff is the GTE load-delay nop; the register  pin is NOT load-bearing (cheat-stripped sandbox emits no spurious move, GCC returns in v0 naturally). Clean finished form = tier-1 file-scope __asm__(glabel) canonical-asm block, identical treatment to authorized siblings func_8007F87C/func_8007F5EC/motutil_GetWalkDir in inline_asm_canonical.txt (user-authorized 2026-05-13). Sibling func_8007F12C (avsz4) is the identical case -> authorize as a cluster.

Sole tier-3 debt is one __asm__(nop) compensating for the maspsx .L-label load-delay blind spot, LOAD-CONSUMER variant: target has 'lw a1,0(a1); .L<loop-top>:; lw v1,0(a1)' (pointer load, then deref consuming a1 as base across the loop-top label). sandbox --disable all = 0 and the narrow maspsx gate that emits the nop (extending the existing jalr-consumer gate in _handle_nop_before_next_instruction to line_loads_from_reg consumers) makes it 100% pure C -- BUT verify-oracle --rebuild FAILS: the same gate inserts a nop in saTan4FireDisp (text1a.c) that shifts its index-anchored asmfix delete_between (#nop.*'.L279:'), giving 'symbol .L279 already defined'. This is exactly the cascade memory maspsx-is-label-dot-prefix documents as 'not worth the corpus-wide churn'. Clean retirement needs user sign-off: either re-derive saTan4FireDisp's rule cluster, or a per-function-scoped maspsx gate. Both source + maspsx reverted; oracle re-verified green (SHA1 62efab4f).
